title: Yoga Instructor
description: A yoga instructor in India typically teaches yoga classes in gyms, studios, or private settings. They design and lead yoga sessions for students of various skill levels, often focusing on physical postures, breathing techniques, and meditation. In addition to teaching, they may also conduct workshops, events, and online sessions, and sometimes offer private coaching or therapy services.
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Yoga, New Delhi
Swami Vivekananda Yoga Anusandhana Samsthana, Bengaluru
Sri Sathya Sai Institute of Higher Medical Sciences, Puttaparthi

career_progression.year_0_2: Initial years involve teaching yoga classes, workshops, and events. You may also assist senior instructors or work as a yoga therapist.
career_progression.year_3_5: As you gain experience, you can lead your own classes, workshops, and events. You may also start offering private coaching or therapy services.
career_progression.year_6_10: You can become a senior instructor, leading large classes and workshops. You may also start your own yoga studio or business.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: You can start a yoga studio, online platform, or product business, leveraging your expertise and network.
career_progression.global_pathway: You can work as a yoga instructor in international yoga retreats, studios, or resorts, or teach yoga abroad through online platforms.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting out as a yoga instructor can be challenging, with limited income and uncertain job security.
common_misconceptions: Yoga is a hobby, not a career
Yoga instructors are not respected professionals
